description: Maps Fiserv/First Data statement fee labels to canonical network fees with published reference rates. Built from official Visa, Mastercard, Discover, and Amex fee schedules cross-referenced against actual Fiserv merchant statements. This is the internal reference asset that replaces the non-existent 'Fiserv pass-through fee schedule'.

proof_statuses.proven_at_cost: Charged amount matches reference rate within tolerance
proof_statuses.rate_exceeds_reference: Charged amount > reference rate + tolerance — possible padding
proof_statuses.not_proof_eligible: No single public reference rate exists (FANF tiers, BIN fees, interchange programs)
proof_statuses.indeterminate: Reference exists but statement data insufficient to verify (missing auth count, volume split, etc.)
proof_statuses.processor_controlled: Fee is processor markup — no network reference rate applies

matching_order: 1. Identify card-type section header (MASTERCARD, MC OFLN DB, VISA, VS OFLN DB, AMEXCT043, DCVR ACQ)
2. Normalize fee description (uppercase, strip extra whitespace, remove OCR artifacts)
3. Match against fiserv_labels for the correct network
4. If matched, apply verification_formula using statement volume/count/rate
5. Compare charged amount vs expected amount within tolerance_pct

category_definitions.interchange: Pass-through to card-issuing bank. Non-negotiable. Set by networks.
category_definitions.processor_markup: Processor/ISO revenue. Negotiable. The merchant's primary cost-reduction lever.
category_definitions.processor_misc: Fixed/monthly processor fees. Negotiable. Often junk fees.
category_definitions.pin_debit_network: PIN debit network fees (STAR, Accel, NYCE, Pulse). Semi-negotiable.

fiserv_section_headers.AUTHS & AVS: Sub-section for authorization-related fees (appears under each card type)

fees.applies_to: All US domestic Mastercard authorizations (credit + signature debit)
fees.notes: Unchanged since July 2013. $0.0295 for non-domestic auths (eff. Apr 2024). Verification: count × $0.0195 = charged amount.

fees.applies_to: All Mastercard debit (offline/signature) gross sales volume
fees.notes: 0.13% on transactions < $1,000. 0.14% on transactions >= $1,000 for credit. Fiserv often lumps credit+debit under same label per card-type section — use the section header (MC OFLN DB vs MASTERCARD) to determine rate.

fees.applies_to: All Mastercard credit gross sales volume (< $1,000 per txn). 0.14% for txns >= $1,000.
fees.notes: Same label as debit on Fiserv statements. Disambiguate by card-type section header. Rate is 0.13% for txns < $1,000, 0.14% for >= $1,000.

fees.applies_to: All Mastercard gross transaction volume
fees.notes: 0.0075% of volume. Exact rate varies slightly by acquirer's total MC volume tier, but 0.0075% is the standard published rate. Some acquirers show 0.05% (0.0005) on statements — this may include bundled fees or acquirer padding. The Abdul Basher statement shows 0.0005 (0.05%), which is significantly above the published 0.0075%. Flag for review.

fees.applies_to: Each merchant location accepting Mastercard. Typically billed in May for prior year.
fees.notes: $1.25/location/month is the published rate. Some processors bill annually in May. The Abdul Basher statement shows $0.62, which could be a partial-month or different billing cycle. Verify against location count.

fees.applies_to: Each Mastercard authorization submitted for clearing
fees.notes: Rate is technically per kilobyte of data transmitted, but effectively works out to ~$0.0023 per auth. Actual amount depends on data payload size. Fiserv shows it as a per-auth fee with a rate like 0.00229.

fees.canonical_name: BIN ICA Fee (Interbank Card Association Service Fee)
fees.applies_to: Mastercard BIN-level service fee
fees.notes: Small per-item or volume-based fee. Exact rate depends on BIN and transaction type. Typically very small amounts ($0.01–$0.15). Not publicly published at a single rate.

fees.applies_to: Mastercard card-not-present sales volume (consumer credit, commercial, signature debit)
fees.notes: 0.01% of CNP volume. Only applies to card-not-present transactions. Will not appear on CP-only merchant statements.

fees.applies_to: US-acquired transactions with non-US issued card, settled in USD
fees.notes: 0.60% domestic (USD settlement). 1.00% foreign (non-USD settlement).

fees.applies_to: All US-based Visa credit card authorizations
fees.notes: Credit = $0.0195. Debit = $0.0155. Fiserv often uses one label for both — disambiguate by card-type section (VISA vs VS OFLN DB). PIN debit is excluded.

fees.applies_to: All US-based Visa signature debit authorizations (not PIN debit)
fees.notes: Same Fiserv label as credit APF. Disambiguate by section header (VS OFLN DB = debit). Rate is lower than credit ($0.0155 vs $0.0195).

fees.applies_to: All merchants accepting Visa, per TIN. CP: tiered by location count. CNP: tiered by monthly Visa volume.
fees.notes: Card-present single-location: ~$2–$5/month. Rate varies by MCC, location count, volume tier. No single reference rate — use tiered schedule. Exempt if monthly Visa CP volume < $200. Fiserv label 'FIXED NETWORK CP FEE' = card-present FANF.
fees.verification_formula: not_proof_eligible — tiered, requires location + MCC context

fees.applies_to: All Visa credit card gross sales volume
fees.notes: 0.14% of credit volume. Previously 0.13% before Jan 2019.

fees.applies_to: All Visa debit card gross sales volume
fees.notes: 0.13% of debit volume.

fees.applies_to: All Visa transactions (sales, returns, reversals, chargebacks)
fees.notes: $0.0018 per transaction. Very small. Fiserv label is often 'FILE TRANSMISSION FEE'. The Abdul Basher statement shows $0.10 flat — this is likely a lump or may include bundled items. Cross-check against transaction count.

fees.applies_to: Visa debit/prepaid transactions that do not meet CPS (Custom Payment Service) requirements
fees.notes: $0.10 per non-qualifying debit/prepaid transaction. Penalty fee for not meeting CPS data requirements. Should be rare for well-configured terminals.

fees.applies_to: All Visa transactions where the card is issued outside the US
fees.notes: 0.45% flat per international transaction. Fiserv label varies: INTERNTL ACQUIRER FEE, INTL ACQUIRER FEE. Range is 0.3%–2.3% depending on settlement currency and region.

fees.applies_to: International debit card authorizations acquired in US
fees.notes: Applies to non-US debit cards. Small per-item fee. Not widely published at a fixed rate.

fees.applies_to: International Visa transactions — additional acquirer-level ISA
fees.notes: Separate from the main ISA. Rate varies. Abdul Basher statement shows 1.017% on $10.81 international volume. Cross-border fees can stack.

fees.applies_to: Transactions settled without required authorization or with auth/clearing mismatch
fees.notes: $0.09 penalty per misuse. Should be rare. If appearing frequently, terminal/gateway misconfiguration.

fees.applies_to: Visa ecommerce/digital commerce transactions
fees.notes: 0.0075% with a $0.0075 minimum per transaction.

fees.applies_to: All Amex OptBlue sale transactions
fees.notes: 0.165% of Amex volume. Some sources show 0.15%. Use 0.165% as current reference. Fiserv label is typically just 'NETWORK FEE' under the AMEX section.

fees.applies_to: All Amex OptBlue authorized transactions
fees.notes: IMPORTANT: Published rate is $0.02/txn. The Abdul Basher statement shows $0.04/txn — this is DOUBLE the published rate and is strong evidence of processor padding. Flag as rate_exceeds_reference whenever rate > $0.025.

fees.applies_to: Amex OptBlue interchange equivalent — varies by industry, ticket size, card type
fees.notes: Amex OptBlue wholesale discount rates are not a single number — they vary by MCC, average ticket, and program (Retail, Restaurant, B2B, etc.). Treat as interchange equivalent. Not proof-eligible at a single rate.

fees.applies_to: All Amex card-not-present (ecommerce, MOTO) transactions
fees.notes: 0.30% on all CNP volume, in addition to the 0.165% assessment. Total CNP assessment = 0.465%.

fees.applies_to: All Discover authorized transactions
fees.notes: $0.0195/txn. Some sources show $0.0025 as the Network Auth Fee (separate). Fiserv often shows this as 0.00250 rate — verify which fee is being represented. The Abdul Basher statement shows $0.0025 × 1 txn = $0.00 (rounded to zero).

fees.applies_to: All Discover authorized transactions
fees.notes: $0.0025/auth. Separate from Data Usage Fee. Both may appear on same statement. Abdul Basher shows $0.01 for 1 auth at 0.01000 rate — this is 4× the published rate. Possible padding or rate change.

fees.applies_to: All Discover gross sales volume
fees.notes: 0.13% of volume. Fiserv uses same 'DUES & ASSESSMENTS' label as MC — disambiguate by card-type section (DCVR ACQ).

fees.rate_unit: varies by program, card type, MCC
fees.applies_to: All card transactions — paid to the card-issuing bank
fees.notes: Interchange is a single lump line in the Fees Charged section but is broken out in the Interchange Charges/Program Fees detail table. Cross-check: the sum of INTERCHANGE lines in Fees Charged should ≈ the total in the detail table. If they don't match, the processor may be padding interchange (a known ISO trick).
fees.verification_formula: Sum of INTERCHANGE fee lines should match Interchange Detail Table total. Any delta > $0.50 is suspicious.

fees.applies_to: Processor markup on all volume, applied per card-type section
fees.notes: This is the processor's basis-point markup. On interchange-plus pricing, appears as a uniform rate across all card types (e.g., 0.20% in the Abdul Basher statement). On tiered pricing, DISC 1/2/3 correspond to qualified/mid-qualified/non-qualified tiers. Key identifier: rate is identical across all card-type sections = IC+ markup. Rates vary = tiered.
fees.verification_formula: Always processor_controlled. Benchmark: competitive IC+ is 0.05%–0.15% for high volume, 0.15%–0.30% for small merchants.

fees.applies_to: Processor per-item markup on each transaction, applied per card-type section
fees.notes: Processor's per-item fee. In the Abdul Basher statement: $0.10/item across all card types. This stacks with CPU GTWY and SALES ITEMS fees — the merchant is effectively paying $0.30/transaction in per-item processor fees. Benchmark: competitive is $0.05–$0.10 total per-item (not stacked).

fees.applies_to: Per-authorization gateway fee charged by processor
fees.notes: Processor gateway fee. Abdul Basher: $0.10/auth across all networks. This is a second per-item fee that stacks on top of OTHER ITEM FEES. Sometimes legitimately a third-party gateway cost, but on Fiserv statements it's typically processor revenue. Benchmark: $0.02–$0.05 is competitive for direct processor gateway.

fees.applies_to: Additional processor percentage markup, often on Amex volume
fees.notes: Processor volume markup. Abdul Basher: 0.30% on Amex volume ($64.87). This is separate from DISC 1 and is additional processor revenue.

fees.applies_to: Fee per batch settlement submitted
fees.notes: Abdul Basher: $0.35/batch × 20 batches = $7.00. Benchmark: $0.10–$0.25 is competitive. This encourages merchants to batch less often (once daily), which some processors exploit.

fees.applies_to: Third per-item fee charged by processor, stacking on OTHER ITEM FEES and CPU GTWY
fees.notes: Abdul Basher: $0.10/item × 64 items = $6.40. This is the THIRD per-item fee stacking on top of OTHER ITEM FEES ($0.10) and CPU GTWY ($0.10). Total per-item markup = $0.30. This triple-stacking is a known processor pricing trick.

fees.applies_to: Monthly account/statement fee
fees.notes: Abdul Basher: $10.00/month. Pure processor revenue. Benchmark: $5–$10 typical, $0 on competitive accounts. Often waivable in negotiations.

fees.applies_to: Processor compliance/regulatory junk fee
fees.notes: Abdul Basher: $3.95/month. This is a 'junk fee' — pure processor revenue disguised with a compliance-sounding name. No corresponding network or regulatory mandate requires this specific fee. Fully negotiable, often removable.

fees.applies_to: PCI DSS compliance program fee or non-compliance penalty
fees.notes: Ranges from $5–$15/month for compliance to $19.95–$49.95/month for non-compliance. Compliance is real but the fee amount is processor-controlled. The actual cost of the merchant's SAQ validation is ~$0. Non-compliance penalties are pure markup.

fees.applies_to: Annual account fee
fees.notes: Ranges from $49–$299/year. Pure processor revenue. Often waivable. Some ISOs disguise this as a 'technology fee' or 'platform fee'.

fees.canonical_name: STAR PIN Debit Network Annual Fee
fees.applies_to: Annual fee for STAR PIN debit network access, per active merchant location
fees.notes: Increasing by $2.00 to $20.95 effective October 2025 statement. Active location = any unique address/domain that processed ≥1 STAR transaction during June–August 2025. Announced in Abdul Basher's August 2025 statement notice.

fees.canonical_name: Accel PIN Debit Network Annual Fee
fees.applies_to: Annual fee for Accel PIN debit network access, per active merchant location
fees.notes: Increasing by $2.00 to $21.95 effective October 2025 statement. Same active-location definition as STAR.

fees.applies_to: Charged when merchant closes account before contract end date
fees.notes: Ranges from $295–$595 flat, or 'liquidated damages' based on remaining contract months × average monthly fees. Some contracts have $0 ETF. Always negotiable at contract signing.

fees.applies_to: Charged when total monthly fees fall below a contractual minimum
fees.notes: Typically $25–$50/month. Only charged as the difference between actual fees and the minimum. Example: $25 minimum, $18 in actual fees → $7 minimum fee charged.
